item.title: Topolski's Chronicle, V.4 No.15, 1956
item.creator: Topolski, Feliks, 1907-1989
item.description: Topolski's Chronicle is a series of broadsheets created by Feliks Topolski and distributed bimonthly to subscribers from 1953 through 1982. Topolski was a superlative draughtsman and covered current events with an artistic, unique style.

pages.typeset_text:
UNIVERSITY OF UTAH LIBRARIES
TOPOLSKI's CHRONICLE No. 15 (75) Vol. IV 1956
...nothing affirmeth and therefore never lyeth... Sir Philip Sidney (1580)
MONTREAL, CANADA
pages.handwritten_text:
French catholic priest from St Joseph Oratory St George's Westmount, Montreal (English private school)
'Montreal Reflect.'
'Santa Feastball'
'please 1.50'
sketch_descriptions: A sketch of a figure in a long cloak and hat, standing in front of a fence with several other figures in various poses behind it; the handwritten note identifies the main figure as a French Catholic priest from St Joseph Oratory, with reference to St George's school in Westmount, Montreal.

A landscape sketch showing a hill with a large cross at the top and what appears to be a small building or chapel; in the foreground are groups of people, with one figure carrying a sign stating 'please 1.50'. The handwritten notes suggest it is a scene called 'Montreal Reflect.' and 'Santa Feastball'.
pages.page_summary: This 1956 page from Topolski's Chronicle features pen sketches depicting scenes and people in Montreal, Canada, including a French Catholic priest from St Joseph Oratory and a landscape with a hilltop cross and groups of people.

pages.handwritten_text: "At 400" Felix Topolski, Montreal 1956 "Au Sczicé" Montreal East FRENCH-Canadian Entertainments "Riviera Espresso-Café" Reb - Indian Boheme French Canadian & Polish Celebrities (?)  A. M. ???  University of McGill School of Architecture
sketch_descriptions: A group of people wearing hats and engaged in various activities, including reading and conversing, annotated as 'At 400' in Montreal, 1956.
A lively scene with several people engaged in conversation, including a man with glasses, in a setting labeled 'Au Sczicé' in Montreal East with French-Canadian entertainment.

Portrait-style sketches of several individuals, including a man with glasses and a woman pointing, annotated with 'Riviera Espresso-Café' and references to 'Reb - Indian Boheme' and French Canadian and Polish celebrities.
A group of individuals engaged in a discussion or study around tables with architectural models, labeled 'University of McGill School of Architecture'.
pages.page_summary: This page contains multiple sketches by Felix Topolski depicting social scenes and individuals in Montreal in 1956, including French-Canadian cultural settings and scenes at McGill University's School of Architecture.

pages.typeset_text: "TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE" IS PUBLISHED 24 TIMES A YEAR AT 3s. 6d. (50c) A COPY YEARLY SUBSCRIPTION RATES (WITH FOLDER AND INDEX) TO ANY PART OF THE WORLD ARE 4s. 6d. (12 or equivalent): SHORTER PERIODS PRO RATA. AIR MAIL BY QUOTATION. SUBSCRIPTIONS FROM OUTSIDE THE STERLING AREA MAY BE PAID THROUGH THE UNESCO COUPON SCHEME Handprinted in Great Britain by Poets' & Painters' Press 146 Sutton Walk, S.E.1. from blocks made by Austin Miles Ltd and published from Bridge Arch 158. Sutton Walk. London, S.E.1. Copyright reserved.
pages.handwritten_text:
Notre Dame De Boulogne point pour Paris
A large Captain America [?]
French Canadians Farmers de la Fruit Market
At East End with East European new arrivals Felix Topolski
Another another tomb at Ulster takes place in Oration at St Joseph in Calcutta + M. G. [?]
sketch_descriptions: Sketch of Notre Dame de Boulogne area with detailed architecture and two figures near water.
Scene showing French Canadian farmers at a fruit market with figures in coats and hats.
Street scene in the East End with East European new arrivals, showing buildings with stairs and several people, including children and adults.
Depiction of a religious exposition at a basilique, with figures descending steps and two seated individuals in the foreground.
pages.page_summary: This page contains four pen sketches depicting various urban and market scenes including Notre Dame de Boulogne, an East End street with new arrivals, a French Canadian fruit market, and a religious exposition at a basilique, accompanied by handwritten notes and publication details.
